note: NOMINAL contemporaneous price, split_factor 1 (PFE has had no stock split; the only corporate action is the Nov-2020 Upjohn/Viatris spin-off, which is NOT a split and does not change the share-for-share price). Modern feeds dividend-adjust history, so the raw close (which EXCLUDES dividend adjustment) is what recovers the contemporaneous nominal price; raw_close(last <= as_of) x 1 = $51.70, reconciling exactly to the card. Independent corroboration that does NOT come from the price feed: Q3-2021 10-Q share count (5,612,866,598, S011) x card market cap $290B implies $51.67/sh (0.06%), plus dated 2022-02-08 reaction coverage (CNBC/Fortune/Nasdaq: stock down ~5-6% on a 2022 guide of ~$102B vs ~$106B consensus, with ~$54B = $32B Comirnaty + $22B Paxlovid). This follows the NVDA exemplar pattern.

live_fields.low_high_hug_reason: Not a hug: $51.70 sits mid-band, +55.0% off the trailing-52wk nominal low ($33.36) and -16.2% off the nominal high ($61.71). This is a normal post-print entry (stock fell ~5-6% on the disappointing 2022 COVID guide), not an extreme-of-series grab, so no justification is required and T2 passes on band distance.

sources.note: raw close 51.70 x split_factor 1 (no PFE split; raw close excludes dividend adjustment) -> nominal $51.70; validator refetched_yahoo 51.70 (delta 0.00%)
independent of the price feed; $290B / 5.6129B sh = $51.67 (0.06% vs card)
dated as_of reaction: stock down ~5-6%; 2022 guide ~$102B vs ~$106B est; $54B COVID ($32B Comirnaty + $22B Paxlovid) corroborates the as_of level and setup
trailing-~372d intraday high x split_factor 1 (Dec-2021 ATH region)
trailing-~372d intraday low x split_factor 1 (early-2021 trough)
5,612,866,598 common shares outstanding as of 2021-10-03 (latest public count before as_of)

live_fields.value: As of 2022-02-08: FY2022 guidance ~$98-102B revenue / adj EPS $6.35-6.55 (midpoint ~$6.45), INCLUDING ~$32B Comirnaty + ~$22B Paxlovid (~$54B, ~54% of revenue) -- the COVID windfall whose post-2022 durability is the binding question. The guide came in BELOW the ~$106-108.6B sell-side consensus, which is why the stock fell. Do NOT use the actual FY2022 print or any post-as_of revision.

sources.note: Zacks/Nasdaq + FXEmpire: guide below ~$108.6B consensus -> grim 2022 sales view, stock down

live_fields.value: As of 2022-02-08: the ~$54B 2022 COVID windfall (Comirnaty + Paxlovid) is the load-bearing variable and its post-2022 run-rate is OPENLY UNSETTLED in the as_of record -- as-of sell-side (SVB Leerink, S007 2021-11-23) already modeled Paxlovid as 'seasonal from 2023' and debated normalization. The company does not cleanly break out COVID profit, so ex-COVID earnings power (~$2.50-3.50 adj EPS) is an as_of estimate, not a disclosed figure. This is the false_cheap_value_trap framing: the optical ~8x forward P/E is a peak-windfall artifact. NOTHING about the actual post-2022 COVID decline is used (that is after as_of).

sources.note: pre-as_of sell-side: ~$101B 2022 on COVID; Paxlovid seasonal from 2023 -> durability question is contemporaneous, not hindsight
2022 guide concentrates ~$54B in two COVID products; ex-COVID base ~$46B grows ~6%

live_fields.value: As of 2022-02-08: dividend is INTACT and was just RAISED to $0.40/quarter ($1.60 annualized, ~3.1% yield at $51.70), the 333rd consecutive quarterly dividend, declared 2021-12-10 (S010). Well-covered even on normalized ex-COVID earnings. This is why the case is a value trap (survivable, paid-to-wait), NOT a melting ice cube. No buybacks planned (per call, S003).

sources.note: Q1-2022 dividend $0.40/qtr ($1.60 ann.), 333rd consecutive, raised; record/payable dates set
management: dividend priority, no buybacks; $25B BD ambition (unexecuted)
